key_insight: MergeSurv shows that sequentially merging task-specific pathology models can support continual survival prediction on whole-slide images without storing prior data.
clinical_relevance: This could help pathology teams adapt prognosis models across cancer cohorts more efficiently while preserving privacy and reducing retraining costs.
method: Fine-tune a foundation model separately on each cancer cohort/task.
Merge learned parameters sequentially into one unified model.
Compare One-for-All (OFA) and Voting-Expert Aggregation (VEA) inference strategies.
evidence_signal: In experiments on four TCGA cohorts, MergeSurv outperformed naive fine-tuning and representative continual learning baselines while reducing catastrophic forgetting.
watch_item: Performance and stability may depend on how well parameter merging transfers across cohorts, so deployment across very different tumor types may need careful validation.
paper_title: MergeSurv: Merging-Based Continual Learning for Survival Analysis on Whole-Slide Images
